title: noccaea montana subsp. arctica
intro: Noccaea montana subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic penny cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family that is native to the Arctic tundra regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Noccaea montana subsp. arctica is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be used in salves or creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
